stdin_parsed.last_assistant_message:
Partially. Fix solves NAT-vs-app-host confusion for the *current* running instance. But that log shows a different problem: **multiple historical app-host instance IDs** (`i-067986ee62b1939f1`, `i-0db523eda47b8f1db`, `i-05ddc3493c6aa29c3`) — the agent didn't know which was "the" instance it should follow.

The `describe-instances` command I added includes `stopped` state, so it'd return all of those and could recreate the same confusion.

Better fix: add a second variant that sorts by launch time and picks the most recent, plus guidance to anchor on one instance ID for the whole diagnostic session:

```bash
# Most recent app-host instance (use this ID for the whole session)
rtk aws --region us-east-1 --profile personal-private-research-AdministratorAccess ec2 describe-instances \
  --filters "Name=tag:Name,Values=app-host" \
  --query "sort_by(Reservations[].Instances[], &LaunchTime)[-1].[InstanceId,State.Name,LaunchTime,InstanceType]" \
  --output table
```

Want me to add that to the skill with an explicit "anchor on this instance ID for the rest of the session" instruction?
